Garbage Disposal Services in Chicago, IL

Licensed Plumbing Professionals • Available 24/7

Call 833-758-6911
Home › Services › Garbage Disposal Services
Licensed & Insured 24/7 Service Serving Chicagoland

Garbage Disposal Installation & Repair

A working garbage disposal makes kitchen cleanup easier. A broken one turns your sink into a swamp. We install new disposals and repair existing ones throughout Chicagoland — from basic 1/3 HP units to heavy-duty 1 HP models for family kitchens.

Common Disposal Problems

  • Jammed: Utensil, bone, or hard object stuck in the grinding chamber.
  • Won't turn on: Tripped reset button, blown fuse, or burned-out motor.
  • Leaking: From sink flange (top), discharge pipe (side), or bottom (internal seal failure).
  • Slow draining: Partial clog in the disposal or drain line downstream.
  • Loud grinding: Loose impellers, worn bearings, or debris in the chamber.

Typical Garbage Disposal Costs

  • Repair (jam, reset, drain clearing): $100–$200
  • Replacement (standard 1/2 HP): $200–$400 including unit and labor
  • Replacement (premium 3/4–1 HP): $350–$600 including unit and labor
  • New installation (no existing disposal): $250–$500 including unit, wiring, and drain

If your disposal is humming but not spinning, or leaking from the bottom, replacement is usually more cost-effective than repair.

Frequently Asked Questions

What size garbage disposal do I need?

1–2 people: 1/3 to 1/2 HP. Families of 3–4: 1/2 to 3/4 HP. Heavy cooks or large families: 3/4 to 1 HP. Bigger motors grind faster and jam less.

Can I put a disposal in my condo?

Check your building's rules — some older Chicago condos prohibit disposals due to aging drain lines. If allowed, we can install one.

Why does my disposal smell bad?

Food residue on the splash guard and grinding chamber. Run cold water and ice cubes through to clean. Citrus peels help with odor. Persistent smells may indicate a drain line clog below.

How long do garbage disposals last?

8–12 years with normal use. If yours is approaching that age and starting to jam, leak, or grind slowly, replacement beats repair.

Can you connect my disposal to my dishwasher drain?

Yes. Most dishwashers drain through the disposal. We remove the knockout plug and connect the dishwasher drain hose securely.

Need Garbage Disposal Services?

Our licensed plumbers are available 24/7 for emergency and scheduled service.
